title: PacketDatagramChannel._read_packets swallows StopAsyncIteration incorrectly
description:
File: python/src/lichen/client/packet_coap.py, lines 344-346

The _read_packets method iterates over packets without handling exceptions:

```python
async def _read_packets(self) -> None:
    async for packet in self._packet_transport.packets():
        self._handle_packet(packet)
```

Issue 1: If _handle_packet raises an exception, _read_packets terminates silently (the task just ends) and no more packets are processed. The caller has no way to detect this failure.

Issue 2: If packets() raises any exception (not StopAsyncIteration), the reader task dies without notifying anyone. The channel appears open but stops receiving.

Impact: A single malformed packet that causes an unhandled exception in _handle_packet (e.g., from code outside the try/except) will break the entire channel silently.

Fix: Wrap the loop body in try/except and log errors, or notify the channel owner:
```python
async def _read_packets(self) -> None:
    try:
        async for packet in self._packet_transport.packets():
            try:
                self._handle_packet(packet)
            except Exception:
                logger.exception('unhandled error in _handle_packet')
    except Exception:
        logger.exception('packet reader failed')
        self.close()
```
